behavior_summary: Provides logic for player interactions with a gravestone object: examining ('U8P_GRAVE_EW::look') produces a brief descriptive message and using ('use') delegates a more complex action to a namespaced handler.
GRAVE_EW::look constructs a short string beginning with "gravestone ", delivers it to the examining process via Item::bark, frees the temporary string, and returns.

GRAVE_EW::use records object/process context (set-info / process-exclude behavior inferred) and asynchronously spawns GRAVE_NS::00CB so the spawned handler performs the concrete 'use' behavior with knowledge of who activated the gravestone.
Both handlers perform local context housekeeping (info/process-exclude opcodes observed) before calling external routines and then inspect return values to influence final control flow.
notable_control_flow: GRAVE_EW::look runs synchronously: build-string -> Item::bark(target_pid, constructed_string) -> free string -> check retval -> implied/pop -> suspend/return.

GRAVE_EW::use runs: set object/process info and exclude the triggering process -> spawn GRAVE_NS::00CB with caller_pid (asynchronous delegation) -> check spawn retval -> implies/pop -> suspend/return.
No global flags are read or written by these snippets; behavior is contained to the object and spawned handler.
open_questions: Exact semantics of the observed 'set info' and 'process exclude' opcodes: which info key is set and what precise effect 'process exclude' has on subsequent engine processing is inferred but not explicit.
What GRAVE_NS::00CB (the spawned handler) actually does when a gravestone is 'used' — this file delegates the behavior so the gameplay effect is unknown without that namespaced script.
Whether the constructed string appended any dynamic gravestone-specific text (owner name, date, epitaph) in addition to the literal "gravestone " is not explicit in this snippet.

main_function.name: U8P_GRAVE_EW::look
main_function.purpose: When a player/actor examines the gravestone, present a short descriptive text (starts with "gravestone ") to the examiner and perform local context bookkeeping before returning.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_GRAVE_EW::look) — triggered by the player/actor examining or 'looking at' the gravestone object.

function_map.name: U8P_GRAVE_EW::look
function_map.role: Handle 'U8P_GRAVE_EW::look' (examine) interactions: construct and deliver a brief description to the examining process.

function_map.name: U8P_GRAVE_EW::use
function_map.role: Handle 'use' (activate) interactions: set object/process context and spawn GRAVE_NS::00CB to perform asynchronous use behavior.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine-provided routine to send/display a short text string on behalf of an item/process to a target process.

function_map.name: spawn GRAVE_NS::00CB
function_map.role: Engine/spawn operation to asynchronously start a handler in the GRAVE_NS namespace to process a 'use' action.

internal_calls.purpose: Delegate the heavy lifting of the 'use' action to a namespaced asynchronous handler so the gravestone's use behavior runs separately and with the caller_pid context.

external_calls.purpose: Deliver the constructed description string (starting with 'gravestone ') to the examining process (caller_pid).
Asynchronously start the GRAVE_NS::00CB handler and pass the caller_pid so the spawned process knows who activated the gravestone.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides logic for player interactions with a gravestone object: examining ('U8P_GRAVE_EW::look') produces a brief descriptive message and using ('use') delegates a more complex action to a namespaced handler.
